channel.title: Digital Assets Morning Wire
channel.description: Daily digital-asset regulatory and market digest. AI-sourced and AI-generated — verify against official sources before acting.

item.title: H1 2026 Crypto Hacks Reach Record High as Losses Fall Below USD 1 Billion
item.description: TRM Labs reported that the number of cryptocurrency hacks hit a record high in the first half of 2026, even though total losses fell below one billion United States dollars. So what: More frequent hacks raise the odds that client funds or counterparty exchanges are affected, so stolen-fund tracing alerts and exchange due-diligence reviews deserve continued attention. [single source]

item.title: OFAC Sanctions 134 ISKP Cryptocurrency Addresses Tied to USD 2 Million in Terrorist Financing
item.description: The United States Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) added 134 cryptocurrency addresses linked to Islamic State Khorasan Province to its sanctions list, tied to roughly two million United States dollars in terrorist financing. So what: The newly listed addresses need to reach wallet-screening data promptly, and a retrospective exposure check against them can confirm no historical flows touched client accounts. [corroborated]

item.title: Crypto Threats, Vulnerabilities and What to Do Next
item.description: An ACAMS Today article surveys current criminal threats and control weaknesses in cryptocurrency and suggests practical next steps for compliance teams. So what: A quick horizon-scanning read, useful when refreshing cryptocurrency risk assessments or training material for financial-crime teams. [single source]

item.title: What OFAC’s crypto mixer sanctions mean for financial crime compliance
item.description: An ACAMS Today article explains how the United States Office of Foreign Assets Control's sanctions on cryptocurrency mixing services affect financial-crime compliance programmes. So what: Helpful framing for tightening controls on mixer exposure, including how indirect exposure several transaction hops away should be treated in monitoring and investigations. [single source]

item.title: Operation Prince: Inside a $15 billion crypto fraud takedown
item.description: An ACAMS Today feature goes inside "Operation Prince", a law-enforcement takedown of a cryptocurrency fraud network measuring about fifteen billion United States dollars. So what: Case studies at this scale surface red-flag typologies — romance-investment fraud patterns and mule account networks — worth folding into monitoring scenarios and investigator training. [single source]

item.title: Live markets: Bitcoin and ether ETFs drew inflows on Monday
item.description: Exchange-traded funds holding bitcoin and ether recorded net investor inflows on Monday, according to CoinDesk market data. So what: Steady fund inflows signal continuing institutional participation in digital assets, sustaining the volumes that flow through banks' brokerage, custody and payments relationships. [single source]

item.title: Grayscale says Strategy’s recent bitcoin sales should restore confidence in its financing structure
item.description: Asset manager Grayscale said recent bitcoin sales by Strategy, the largest corporate holder of bitcoin, should reassure investors about the company's financing structure rather than alarm them. So what: Large corporate bitcoin holders adjusting positions can move prices and sentiment sharply, which matters when assessing collateral values and client exposure to bitcoin-treasury companies. [single source]

item.title: Bitcoin's U.S. reserve still a work-in-progress as federal agencies hash it out
item.description: United States federal agencies are still working through legal and operational questions before the planned national bitcoin reserve becomes operational, CoinDesk reports. So what: A sovereign bitcoin reserve would further legitimise state-level holdings, and the unresolved custody and jurisdiction questions mirror those banks face when holding digital assets. [single source]

item.title: President Trump’s strategic bitcoin reserve hits a legal and jurisdictional snag: Bloomberg
item.description: The planned United States strategic bitcoin reserve has hit legal and jurisdictional obstacles between federal agencies, according to a Bloomberg report cited by The Block. So what: The delays highlight unsettled United States federal authority over seized and state-held cryptoassets, with knock-on implications for custody and sanctions policy worth watching. [single source]

item.title: Judge revives fraud claim against Barry Silbert, DCG in Genesis Yield lawsuit
item.description: A United States judge revived a fraud claim against Digital Currency Group and its founder Barry Silbert in a lawsuit over the collapsed Genesis yield programme. So what: Litigation from the 2022-23 crypto lending collapses keeps counterparty-failure risk visible; exposure reviews for clients tied to affected entities may need refreshing as claims progress. [single source]

item.title: Vitalik Buterin proposes ‘Extremely Lean’ Ethereum, shrinking the chain to near-zero state with ZK proofs
item.description: Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin proposed an "extremely lean" redesign that would shrink the blockchain's stored state to near zero using zero-knowledge cryptographic proofs. So what: Protocol changes of this scale would eventually affect how transaction records used in investigations are stored and verified, though any practical impact is years away. [single source]

item.title: Circle’s USDC is leaving Tether behind in the stablecoin volume race, new data from Visa shows
item.description: New data published by Visa shows Circle's USDC stablecoin pulling ahead of rival Tether in on-chain transaction volume by a widening margin. So what: A volume shift toward the more regulation-forward stablecoin issuer changes the counterparty mix in client transaction flows and is useful context for stablecoin risk assessments. [single source]

item.title: President Trump says he’s a ‘big crypto guy,’ while distancing himself from family’s crypto ventures
item.description: United States President Trump described himself as strongly supportive of cryptocurrency while distancing himself from his family's commercial crypto ventures. So what: Top-level political support continues to shape a permissive United States policy direction, while conflict-of-interest questions around official crypto ventures remain a reputational theme to monitor. [single source]

item.title: DeFi protocol Summer.fi halts Lazy Summer vaults after $6 million exploit
item.description: Decentralised-finance protocol Summer.fi suspended its "Lazy Summer" investment vaults after an exploit drained about six million United States dollars. So what: Fresh exploit proceeds typically move fast through mixers and cross-chain bridges, so screening-provider alerts for exposure to the stolen funds are worth watching over the coming days. [single source]

item.title: How the UK's stablecoin rules came together
item.description: Elliptic published an explainer tracing how the United Kingdom's newly finalised stablecoin regulatory regime was developed and what it covers. So what: A concise map of the United Kingdom regime helps benchmark it against the Hong Kong and European Union stablecoin frameworks when advising on cross-border issuance and custody. [single source]

item.title: Michael Saylor's Strategy dramatically ups pace of bitcoin sales, raising $216 million
item.description: Strategy, the software company turned bitcoin holding vehicle led by Michael Saylor, sharply accelerated its bitcoin sales, raising about 216 million United States dollars. So what: The largest corporate bitcoin holder switching from accumulation to sizeable sales is a market-sentiment signal relevant to collateral valuations and exposure to bitcoin-treasury companies. [single source]

item.title: Ripple's preliminary crypto asset provider license in Luxembourg upgraded to fully compliant
item.description: CoinDesk reports that Ripple's preliminary crypto-asset service provider licence in Luxembourg has been upgraded to fully compliant status under the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA). So what: Full authorisation lets Ripple passport crypto services across the European Economic Area, adding a large licensed counterparty to the European payments and custody landscape. [single source]

item.title: Ripple secures full MiCA CASP authorization for crypto services across 30 EEA countries
item.description: Ripple secured full authorisation as a crypto-asset service provider under the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA), allowing it to offer crypto services across thirty European Economic Area countries. So what: A major payments-focused crypto firm gaining authorisation across the whole European Economic Area expands the set of fully licensed counterparties and sharpens comparison with Hong Kong's licensing regime. [corroborated]

item.title: South Korea develops crypto-specific procedures for asset seizures and liquidation: report
item.description: South Korean authorities are developing procedures specifically for seizing and liquidating cryptocurrency in criminal and tax cases, according to a report cited by The Block. So what: Clearer seizure mechanics in a major Asian market offer a template regional peers may follow and affect how frozen-asset requests involving Korean counterparties are executed. [single source]

item.title: How Sovereign Wealth Funds Invest in Digital Assets
item.description: An explainer from The Block describes the channels sovereign wealth funds use to invest in digital assets, from exchange-traded products to direct holdings and venture stakes. So what: Sovereign investors entering digital assets deepen institutional flows through regulated intermediaries — useful context for onboarding and source-of-funds conversations involving state-linked vehicles. [single source]

item.title: Nigel Farage failed to declare funding from crypto gambling figure convicted of fraud: Sunday Times
item.description: The Sunday Times reported that British politician Nigel Farage failed to declare funding from a cryptocurrency gambling figure previously convicted of fraud. So what: Politically exposed persons with undisclosed crypto-linked funding illustrate the adverse-media and source-of-wealth checks that cryptocurrency exposure adds to customer due diligence. [single source]

item.title: OFAC Updates ISIS-Khorasan Sanctions with Over 100 Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description: Chainalysis analysed the United States Office of Foreign Assets Control's update adding more than one hundred cryptocurrency wallet addresses linked to Islamic State Khorasan to its sanctions designations. So what: The attribution detail on the newly designated addresses supports retrospective exposure checks and tuning of sanctions-screening rules for indirect exposure. [single source]

item.title: Coinbase secures UK investment services license to add derivatives and equities trading
item.description: Coinbase obtained a United Kingdom investment services licence allowing it to offer derivatives and equities trading alongside its existing crypto services. So what: A major crypto exchange holding a mainstream United Kingdom investment licence blurs the line between crypto and traditional brokerage — relevant when classifying counterparties and comparing licensing scope across jurisdictions. [corroborated]

item.title: Coinbase secures UK authorization to offer traditional investments alongside crypto
item.description: CoinDesk reports Coinbase received United Kingdom authorisation to offer traditional investment products alongside cryptocurrency trading. So what: Confirms the same United Kingdom authorisation reported by The Block; the combined crypto-plus-securities permission set is the detail worth noting for counterparty assessments. [corroborated]

item.title: Tether’s former CIO seeks buyers for part of his stake in the firm: Bloomberg
item.description: Bloomberg reports that Tether's former chief investment officer is seeking buyers for part of his personal stake in the stablecoin issuer. So what: Secondary sales of stakes in the largest stablecoin issuer can reveal valuation and governance information relevant to stablecoin counterparty risk assessments. [single source]

item.title: Upbit, Naver stock-swap deal faces second delay amid proposed crypto law debate
item.description: The stock-swap deal between South Korean exchange Upbit's operator and internet group Naver faces a second delay while lawmakers debate a proposed cryptocurrency law. So what: South Korea's pending crypto legislation is now directly shaping major corporate transactions — a signal of how quickly the regional regulatory landscape is moving. [single source]

item.title: Two traders sue Polymarket over disputed resolution of Strategy bitcoin sale market
item.description: Two traders sued prediction-market platform Polymarket over how it resolved a market on Strategy's bitcoin sales. So what: Disputes over prediction-market resolutions illustrate the consumer-protection and manipulation questions regulators are beginning to examine in event-contract markets. [single source]

item.title: Binance taps into Bitcoin holders’ hunger for yield with new covered call yield play
item.description: Binance launched a covered-call product that lets bitcoin holders earn yield on their holdings. So what: Yield products on client crypto holdings raise custody, rehypothecation and suitability questions — useful context when assessing exchange counterparties' business models. [single source]

item.title: What the end of MiCA's transitional period means for crypto businesses
item.description: Elliptic explains that the transitional window under the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) closed on 1 July 2026, after which only firms with full authorisation may serve European Union clients. So what: European counterparties still operating on lapsed national registrations are now unauthorised — worth reflecting in counterparty screening and periodic-review triggers. [single source]

item.title: FSTB and HKMA to further unlock potential of distributed ledger technology in Hong Kong's fixed income market
item.description: The Financial Services and the Treasury Bureau and the Hong Kong Monetary Authority announced further measures to expand the use of distributed ledger technology in Hong Kong's fixed-income market. So what: Signals a continuing official push to make tokenised debt mainstream in Hong Kong — expect follow-on guidance touching settlement, custody and record-keeping controls. [official]

item.title: The European Banking Authority consults on a draft methodology for setting fines under the Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA)
item.description: The European Banking Authority is consulting on a draft methodology for setting fines on issuers it supervises under the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation. So what: A concrete preview of how penalties under the EU Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation will be sized — a useful benchmark when gauging the EU enforcement exposure of stablecoin and token issuers. [official]

item.title: The EBA updates validation rules for supervisory reporting
item.description: The European Banking Authority published updated validation rules for supervisory reporting, flagging corrections reporting institutions should apply. So what: Routine, but worth passing to regulatory-reporting teams handling EU entities, including MiCA-related returns. [official]

item.title: Wallet Screening Best Practices for Compliance Teams
item.description: TRM Labs published a guide on how compliance teams should screen cryptocurrency wallets for sanctions and financial-crime risk. So what: A useful benchmark for reviewing whether existing wallet-screening controls cover sanctions exposure, indirect risk and re-screening frequency in line with current industry practice. [single source]

item.title: Kraken wins $22 million arbitration against auditor Mazars for ditching the firm during Operation Choke Point 2.0
item.description: Kraken's parent company Payward won a $22 million arbitration award against its former auditor Mazars, which had abandoned a nearly completed audit in 2022 citing risk from the Securities and Exchange Commission's crackdown on crypto firms during the period known as Operation Choke Point 2.0. So what: The award adds to the documented record of United States banking regulators and enforcement-era pressure pushing professional-services firms to de-risk from digital-asset clients, a useful reference point when weighing today's more permissive supervisory tone toward crypto relationships. [single source]

item.title: CFTC charges North Carolina man over alleged $14 million crypto, futures fraud
item.description: The United States Commodity Futures Trading Commission charged North Carolina resident Trevor Vernon and his firm Argent Capital Management with defrauding about 60 investors of roughly $14 million by falsely claiming trading success across futures, options and cryptocurrency positions, while losing at least $8.6 million of investor funds. So what: A commodity-pool fraud case naming cryptocurrency as one of several traded asset classes is a reminder that unregistered pooled investment vehicles touching digital assets remain a live enforcement priority for United States derivatives regulators, relevant when screening counterparties or referral sources claiming crypto trading expertise. [corroborated]

item.title: BlackRock-backed Securitize slides 40% after SPAC debut despite tokenization boom
item.description: Shares of BlackRock-backed tokenization firm Securitize fell about 40 percent in the days after completing its special-purpose acquisition company (SPAC) merger, a decline analysts attribute to typical post-merger investor turnover rather than any negative development at the company. So what: The share slide is consistent with a broader pattern of recent crypto-linked SPAC listings trading down sharply after debut, a reminder that public-market enthusiasm for tokenization does not necessarily translate into stable valuations for individual platforms. [single source]

item.title: Crypto exchange Kraken is trying to become a bank in Europe
item.description: Crypto exchange Kraken is reportedly pursuing a full banking license in Lithuania that would let it offer current accounts, consumer lending and stock trading across the European Economic Area, though Kraken has not confirmed the plans and the Bank of Lithuania says the licensing process is confidential. So what: A licensed banking arm inside the European Union would materially change Kraken's regulatory footprint and counterparty profile there; worth tracking, as it would be the first such designation held by a cryptocurrency exchange in Europe. [single source]

item.title: Ondo Finance says tokenized stocks can now be used as collateral for perp trading
item.description: Tokenization platform Ondo Finance launched a pre-alpha perpetual futures product, Ondo Perps, that lets users post tokenized versions of United States stocks and exchange-traded funds as collateral for leveraged derivatives positions, unavailable to users in the United States and a short list of restricted jurisdictions. So what: Using tokenized equities as leveraged-derivatives collateral is a new twist on the tokenization trend worth monitoring for how it blends securities-like exposure with crypto-native leverage and cross-border access controls. [single source]

item.title: U.S. SEC to propose crypto rule as soon as this month to ease startups, fundraising
item.description: The United States Securities and Exchange Commission is expected to propose a rule called Regulation Crypto as soon as this month, which Chairman Paul Atkins says would grant temporary registration exemptions and a safe harbor to certain crypto developers raising capital, though the measure remains under White House review before formal publication. So what: If adopted, a new capital-raising exemption regime for crypto issuers would change which digital-asset offerings fall outside standard securities registration, a rule worth tracking closely once formally proposed rather than acting on now. [single source]

item.title: SEC plans crypto rule changes for exchanges and broker dealers in 2026 regulatory agenda
item.description: The United States Securities and Exchange Commission's newly released 2026 regulatory agenda lists planned amendments to broker-dealer minimum capital rules, customer-asset protection rules, and recordkeeping requirements specifically to address how they apply to crypto assets, alongside related exchange-rule changes. So what: These are early-stage agenda items rather than published proposals, but they signal that United States broker-dealer custody and capital treatment of crypto assets is moving toward formal rulemaking, worth flagging for future monitoring of custody and prudential standards affecting broker-dealer counterparties. [corroborated]

item.title: Tether puts $20 million behind Mercado Bitcoin amid Latin America’s tokenization boom
item.description: Stablecoin issuer Tether is investing $20 million in Brazilian digital-financial-services platform Mercado Bitcoin to support its expansion into tokenization, payments, and lending, as part of Tether's broader push into Latin America's digital-asset market. So what: Continued stablecoin-issuer investment in licensed Latin American platforms is a reminder to keep counterparty and beneficial-ownership diligence current on regional partners as stablecoin distribution channels expand there. [single source]

item.title: Former Tether investment chief is looking to sell part of his stake in the stablecoin giant: Bloomberg
item.description: Richard Heathcote, Tether's chief investment officer until March 2026 and now a non-executive advisor, is seeking to sell a small part of his 1.26 percent ownership stake in the stablecoin issuer with the help of advisory firm PJT Partners. So what: A stake sale by a former senior Tether executive is worth noting for ownership-structure and beneficial-owner tracking purposes on one of the largest stablecoin issuers, though the reported sale is described as partial and the value has not been disclosed. [single source]

item.title: Crypto regulatory affairs: UK sets out final stablecoin rules
item.description: The United Kingdom's Financial Conduct Authority published final rules for stablecoin issuers on 30 June 2026, covering backing-asset composition, redemption rights, and custody safeguarding, with the licence application window opening in September 2026 and the full regime becoming binding on 25 October 2027. So what: Any stablecoin issuer or distributor with United Kingdom exposure now has a confirmed rulebook and a concrete compliance deadline to plan against, making this a near-term action item rather than a future policy signal. [corroborated]

item.title: Bitcoin’s early July bounce rides thin summer liquidity as half of supply still sits underwater: analysts
item.description: Bitcoin traded around $63,500 after a six-day rally that recovered from June's low near $57,800, with analysts describing the move as a relief rally driven by softer United States jobs data and dovish Federal Reserve signals rather than a confirmed trend reversal. So what: Pure price-movement commentary with no direct regulatory or compliance angle; useful only as market-context background rather than an item requiring any control or filing action. [single source]

item.title: AMF withdraws AUTOMATA France SAS digital-asset-service-provider registration
item.description: France's securities regulator, the Autorité des marchés financiers, withdrew the digital-asset-service-provider registration of AUTOMATA France SAS (trading as Vancelian), a Board decision effective 30 June 2026, after finding the firm had carried out unlicensed crowdfunding activity that breached senior-manager fitness requirements. This is a national registration withdrawal tied to the wind-down of the transitional Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation registration regime, not a fine issued for a Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation conduct-rule breach. So what: A concrete, recent example of a European Union regulator actively enforcing fitness-and-propriety standards during the transitional Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation registration period is a useful reference point when assessing counterparty and correspondent-relationship risk with European digital-asset-service providers going through their own re-registration. [official]

item.title: Sen. Wyden urges Senate leaders to preserve contested blockchain developer protections in broader crypto bill
item.description: Sen. Wyden urges Senate leaders to preserve contested blockchain developer protections in broader crypto bill So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Binance Wallet adds Plume’s yield vault offering access to Invesco and Bitwise funds
item.description: Binance Wallet adds Plume’s yield vault offering access to Invesco and Bitwise funds So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: European Commission looks to expand MiCA to cover emergence of tokenization, non-EU stablecoin issuers: report
item.description: European Commission looks to expand MiCA to cover emergence of tokenization, non-EU stablecoin issuers: report So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Cardano founding entity EMURGO steps down from Pentad governance role after wallet exploit
item.description: Cardano founding entity EMURGO steps down from Pentad governance role after wallet exploit So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Adam Back's bitcoin treasury firm scraps SPAC merger, seeks new deal
item.description: Adam Back's bitcoin treasury firm scraps SPAC merger, seeks new deal So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Citadel abandons multi-year crypto lawsuit to focus on bankruptcy order against an ex-employee
item.description: Citadel abandons multi-year crypto lawsuit to focus on bankruptcy order against an ex-employee So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: India central bank seeks to bar financial institutions from exposure to crypto assets: Reuters
item.description: India central bank seeks to bar financial institutions from exposure to crypto assets: Reuters So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Reserve Bank of India still favors crypto prohibition to curtail tax evasion: Reuters
item.description: Reserve Bank of India still favors crypto prohibition to curtail tax evasion: Reuters So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: Nigel Farage to quit UK Parliament, seek new mandate amid probes into crypto-linked gifts
item.description: Nigel Farage to quit UK Parliament, seek new mandate amid probes into crypto-linked gifts So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]

item.title: How Financial Institutions Can Build a Strong Crypto Transaction Monitoring Program
item.description: How Financial Institutions Can Build a Strong Crypto Transaction Monitoring Program So what: Review the source directly; automated analysis unavailable. [single source]
